title: Shin Eva Spoilers
wikitext:
[]: Shin Eva Spoilers.             Quote Post by kboyrulez12345 » Tue Dec 29, 2020 11:53 am Just out of curiosity, I'm wondering how many of you are going to read spoilers when the film comes out in Japan, and how many of you are going in for the (probable) long haul to see this film. I'm personally leaning more on waiting, simply for the fact that, when Q first came out, there was a whole bunch of misinformation due to mistranslations and the such that it was hard to tell what's going on. Still, I hope the release of this film isn't anything like Q was for places outside of Japan. Anyway, I'm curious to know what everyone else will be doing.

[]: Re: Shin Eva Spoilers.             Quote Post by Joseki » Tue Dec 29, 2020 12:43 pm I'm gonna stay here until January 22nd 23:59 Tokyo time, then I'm gone.

[]: Re: Shin Eva Spoilers.             Quote Post by ShinjiStranglesAsuka » Tue Dec 29, 2020 12:49 pm Spoilers generally don't ruin things for me so I'll be reading spoilers and watching shitty camrips.

[]: Re: Shin Eva Spoilers.             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Tue Dec 29, 2020 12:54 pm I recall reading spoilers for Q, and still being at a loss for words when the film was over, and downright shocked when Kaworu died

[]: Re: Shin Eva Spoilers.             Quote Post by ShinjiStranglesAsuka » Tue Dec 29, 2020 12:58 pm Too right man. So much of Eva is in the visuals and sounds.

[]: Re: Shin Eva Spoilers.             Quote Post by kboyrulez12345 » Tue Dec 29, 2020 2:53 pm Arcadia's legacy wrote: I recall reading spoilers for Q, and still being at a loss for words when the film was over, and downright shocked when Kaworu died

[]: Re: Shin Eva Spoilers.             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Dec 29, 2020 3:30 pm Joseki wrote: I'm gonna stay here until January 22nd 23:59 Tokyo time, then I'm gone.

[]: Re: Shin Eva Spoilers.             Quote Post by Joseki » Tue Dec 29, 2020 3:34 pm ^ I posted that under the assumption of no leak happening as I personally don't believe they will happen.

[]: Re: Shin Eva Spoilers.             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Dec 29, 2020 3:40 pm Personally, I don't think a leak is highly likely either. But it's not zero, and to quote Simon from Gurren Lagann "As long as it's not zero, it may as well be a hundred percent!" If a leak does happen, do you want to be caught off guard? I don't think so. The way I see it, the simplest course would be to prepare for a leak in the eventuality that it does happen and adjust one's behavior accordingly.

[]: Re: Shin Eva Spoilers.             Quote Post by Thesufferingpumpkin » Tue Dec 29, 2020 9:02 pm Give the fact it’s been over 8 years of waiting and the slim chance of dying of covid right now I’ll be viewing the spoilers and reading up on the reviews. Just in case I don’t live long enough to see it for myself as it will still take a good few months to view it in England, via various incorrect means

[]: Re: Shin Eva Spoilers.             Quote Post by GhostlyOcam » Thu Dec 31, 2020 4:59 am I'm still deciding whether I want to spoil myself right away or wait until further developments on how the film will be available outside Japan. If there's a theatrical release announced in my country (slim chance) or the film being available through alternative means (streaming or home video) relatively fast like 3.0, I'd probably keep myself unspoiled. But I will listen to the soundtrack when it arrives in February because that's like the best way to keep myself hyped for the film while not outright spoiling myself of its story content, since I know the track names aren't like Star Wars where they could easily spoil the film's plot points or key scenes.

[]: Re: Shin Eva Spoilers.             Quote Post by Justacrazyguy » Thu Dec 31, 2020 12:21 pm When 3.0 came around I spoiled myself with little concern. I hadn't even seen the rebuild movies at that point. And then so much time passed that when I actually sat and watched the movie I barely remembered any of the spoilers. I was thinking of doing the same thing, but this being the last movie, I doubt I'll just casually forget stuff like last time. Who knows, but I'm not going to avoid the internet because of a single movie, Eva or not.

[]: Re: Shin Eva Spoilers.             Quote Post by kboyrulez12345 » Thu Dec 31, 2020 12:21 pm GhostlyOcam wrote: I'm still deciding whether I want to spoil myself right away or wait until further developments on how the film will be available outside Japan. If there's a theatrical release announced in my country (slim chance) or the film being available through alternative means (streaming or home video) relatively fast like 3.0, I'd probably keep myself unspoiled. But I will listen to the soundtrack when it arrives in February because that's like the best way to keep myself hyped for the film while not outright spoiling myself of its story content, since I know the track names aren't like Star Wars where they could easily spoil the film's plot points or key scenes.

[]: Re: Shin Eva Spoilers.             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Dec 31, 2020 1:03 pm If GKids/Funimation/Eleven Arts/Fathom Events/whoever were going a simultaneous release Shin Eva with Japan, we'd probably have heard about it by now. They're not just gonna do that unannounced. They would want to advertise for it thread of time to better ensure that they would sell enough tickets to make the expense worth their while.

[]: Re: Shin Eva Spoilers.             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Thu Dec 31, 2020 1:13 pm If it was suddenly announced that 3.0+1.0 was going to get a global release in February or March I might try to be spoiler averse BUT since it could be anywhere from two to eight or nine months after January 23 before I can see a pristine viewing of the film I’m not going to be. In the end for me it’s not the plot but the execution that matters in storytelling.

[]: Re: Shin Eva Spoilers.             Quote Post by kboyrulez12345 » Thu Dec 31, 2020 1:30 pm Yeah, looking at the tweet and remembering that the Japanese language is pretty contextual indicates they were only talking about a Japanese release it seemed. Well, I at least hope the release won't be as long as Q was.

[]: Re: Shin Eva Spoilers.             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Thu Dec 31, 2020 1:39 pm I’m certain everyone who wants to LEGALLY see a pristine viewing of the film will get their chance in 2021. With Toho and Toei involved I don’t see any major shenanigans happening like with 3.0. If it wasn’t for the pandemic I would’ve put money down on an April 2021 theatrical run in Europe and USA at least. Maybe not a full fun but at least a few select showings. With the pandemic it’s really just a question now of HOW we see it. Once Khara handles their domestic release I figure we’ll start hearing news of international distribution.

[]: Re: Shin Eva Spoilers.             Quote Post by Swami » Sat Jan 02, 2021 2:04 am A little disappointed that there have been no leaks from the early premieres. Looks like Khara has done a good job at keeping those people quiet.

[]: Re: Shin Eva Spoilers.             Quote Post by GhostlyOcam » Sat Jan 02, 2021 5:30 am Swami wrote: A little disappointed that there have been no leaks from the early premieres.

[]: Re: Shin Eva Spoilers.             Quote Post by driftking18594 » Sat Jan 02, 2021 7:45 am I'm in two minds about spoiling myself at the moment. I had no problem with 3.0 spoilers, and they had little bearing on how much I enjoyed it when I finally got around to seeing it in July 2013. On the other hand, I remember feeling so deflated (maybe even a tad salty) getting... SPOILER: Show
